hero-title: Make your own little AI helper.
You can make a little AI helper of your own.
Make a little AI helper of your own.
Build your own little AI helper.
hero-lede: This sounds like a job for tech people, but it really isn't. A “helper” is just some clear notes you write for AI. You tell it who to be and how to help you. When you do that well, AI does so much more for you. Don't worry — we'll walk you through it, nice and slow.

It sounds technical, but it really isn't. A “helper” is simply clear instructions you write for AI — telling it who to be and how to help you. Do that well and AI does so much more for you. We'll show you, step by step.

It sounds technical, but it really isn't. A “helper” is just clear instructions you write for AI — telling it who to be and how to help. Do that well and AI does so much more for you. We'll show you, gently.
Sounds technical; isn't. A “helper” is clear instructions you write for AI — who to be, how to help. Done well, AI does so much more. We'll show you.
A “helper” is just clear instructions for AI: who to be, how to help. Done well, it does far more. We'll show you.
reassure: You don't have to be good with computers. You don't have to know any special words. If you can say what you want in plain, everyday English, then you can already do this — and you'll get a little better each time you give it a go.
You don't need to be techy, and you don't need the right words. If you can describe what you want in plain English, you can already do this — and you'll get better each time you try.
You don't need to be techy. You don't need the right words. If you can describe what you want in plain English, you can already do this — and you'll get better every time you try.
No tech skills or special words needed. If you can describe what you want in plain English, you can do this — and you'll improve with each try.
No tech skills needed. Describe what you want in plain English and you can do this — improving each try.
advisor-title: Get much better at asking AI — all in one go
Get much better at asking AI, in one go

Get much better at asking AI, fast
Ask AI better, in one go
advisor-body: Here's the kindest, easiest first step. Paste in something you really did ask AI. Then our free helper shows you how it all works. You get one round of warm, plain-English notes: what you did well, what may have confused the AI, and how to ask so you get a better answer back. It feels a bit like having a kind friend read it over your shoulder.

Here's the friendliest first step: paste in something you actually asked AI, and our free helper shows you how it works. You get a round of kind, plain-English feedback — what was good, what confused it, and how to ask for a better answer. It's like having a patient friend read over your shoulder.

Here's the friendliest first step: paste in something you actually asked AI, and our free helper shows you how it works — a round of kind, plain-English feedback on what was good, what confused it, and how to ask so you get a better answer. It's like having a patient friend read over your shoulder.

The friendliest first step: paste in something you asked AI, and our free helper gives one round of plain-English feedback — what worked, what confused it, and how to ask for a better answer. Like a patient friend reading over your shoulder.
Paste something you asked AI; the free helper returns one round of plain-English feedback — what worked, what confused it, how to ask better.
advisor-preview: Right now, this one is a preview — a worked example for the moment. That means you can see exactly what the feedback looks like before the real, live version is ready. We're being honest: it gives you one round of feedback only, not a back-and-forth chat.

recall-answer: It's being specific. Tell it who it's helping, what it should do, what it shouldn't do, and the kind of answer you'd like back. If you put in something vague, you get something vague out. If you put in something clear, you get something genuinely useful out.
Being specific. Tell it who it's helping, what it should and shouldn't do, and the kind of answer you want back. Vague in means vague out — clear in means genuinely useful out.
Being specific. Tell it who it's helping, what it should and shouldn't do, and the kind of answer you want back. Vague in, vague out — clear in, genuinely useful out.
Being specific. State who it's helping, what it should and shouldn't do, and the answer you want. Vague in, vague out; clear in, useful out.
Specificity. Say who it helps, what to do and avoid, and the answer wanted. Clear in, useful out.
how-title: Three gentle steps. There's no rush at all.
Three gentle steps. No need to rush.
how-sub: Just start at the top. Each step quietly leads you on to the next one, so you don't have to pick or choose.

step1-body: Paste something you asked AI into the free helper, and it gives you friendly notes back. After a few tries, you'll just feel how to ask for the thing you really want.

step2-body: Once you've got the hang of it, chatyman lets you set up a little helper of your own — with no code at all. It's ready whenever you need it, so you don't have to start from scratch each time.
Once you've got the knack, chatyman lets you set up a little helper of your own — no code — that's ready whenever you need it, so you needn't start from scratch each time.

Then chatyman sets up your own no-code helper, ready anytime — no starting from scratch.
step3-body: Want those “aha” moments where it all clicks? A few short, plain lessons explain what's really going on — and they quietly make everything you make turn out better.

someday-body: Some people who get the hang of this go on to make helpers that other folks would love too. We'd like to make sharing them — and even earning a little from them — simple and friendly. We'll be honest with you: that part isn't ready yet. For now, just enjoy making things that help you.

belong-body: Everyone here started out feeling a little unsure, just like you might. We'll send you gentle, no-jargon notes when there's something new worth trying — and you'll find other people working it all out right alongside you.
